user_query:
I want a small, local event-triggered helper that keeps an eye on a drafts folder and, whenever I drop in a new pitch, gives me a quick heads-up about length and any homages to the golden age of television.

Please implement a script at scripts/watch_pitches.py that watches the directory watch/drafts/ for new or modified .md or .txt files. It must also support a one-shot mode (--scan-once) that processes all current .md/.txt files in watch/drafts once and exits. For each file processed, read its text and:
- Calculate word_count, line_count, and estimated_reading_time_min (ceil words/200).
- Detect references to classic TV by matching (case-insensitive, title or alias as whole words) against the provided input/reference_shows.json.
- Write a JSON report for each processed file to output/reports/<basename>_report.json with fields: file (relative path), word_count, line_count, estimated_reading_time_min, references (list of objects with title, era, matched), generated_at (ISO 8601 timestamp). Avoid duplicate references for the same title within a file.
- Append a timestamped line to output/logs/automation.log for each processed file and for any error encountered.

Use only local code and files—no external services or network calls. If watch/drafts/ doesn’t exist, create it and log that event. Make sure your script exits with a non-zero code on failure and zero on success.

Demo run (so I can verify it):
1) Ensure watch/drafts/ exists (create it if needed).
2) Copy input/sample_draft.md into watch/drafts/sample_draft.md.
3) Run your script in one-shot mode to process the sample file, redirecting both stdout and stderr so they are appended to output/logs/automation.log.
4) This must produce output/reports/sample_draft_report.json. In that JSON, the references list must include at least the shows that are present in the sample text: I Love Lucy, The Twilight Zone, and M*A*S*H (titles matched via input/reference_shows.json). Fields must follow the structure above.
5) Create output/last_run_status.txt containing: processed_file: watch/drafts/sample_draft.md, report_path: output/reports/sample_draft_report.json, and exit_code: <the command’s exit code>.

I’ll verify by inspecting the folder structure, examining the log and status files, and opening the generated JSON report. Don’t add extra dependencies; a simple polling loop is fine for the watcher. Please keep paths exactly as specified and do not rename the input/output locations. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
